body:
## Summary

Fixes #771.

The reported session asked *"В каких странах есть частные космические компании?"* and filed a 51KB issue whose transcript was an unreadable wall of scraped website chrome. Two independent defects compose into that symptom, and both are fixed here.

### 1. The research answer dumped the whole fetched page

`web_research::final_answer` passed the fetch tool result through verbatim ([`web_research.rs:79-97`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/main/src/agentic_coding/web_research.rs#L79-L97)):

```rust
format!("{evidence}{source}")   // `evidence` is the entire page
```

So the assistant turn became the site's navigation, menus and every unrelated article instead of an answer to the question.

**Fix:** score the page's sentences by symbolic token overlap with the query — reusing the existing non-neural `symbolic_cosine_similarity` — keep the best few in document order, and cite the source as before. Evidence that is already short stays verbatim, so the concise results covered by #687 keep their existing shape.

### 1a. …and in two scripts it still did, after the first fix

The language-coverage CI guard rejected the first version of this fix, correctly. Exercising the extract in all four supported languages found two scripts where it still returned a page dump, for reasons that only show up outside English:

- **Hindi.** `summarization::formalize` ended a sentence at `.`, `!`, `?`, `。`, `…` or a newline. Hindi prose ends sentences with the danda `।`, which is in none of those, so a Hindi page parsed as **one** statement and "keep the best three sentences" kept the whole document. The danda and double danda `॥` now end a sentence, and `deformalize` no longer appends a full stop after one.
- **Chinese.** `symbolic_cosine_similarity` tokenizes on non-alphanumeric boundaries. Chinese writes without spaces, so a whole sentence is a single token and query and sentence never share one — every candidate scored `0.0` and the extract fell back to the head of the page rather than the part that answered the question. Ranking now falls back to shared-character overlap for CJK queries, following the convention `coding::contains_cjk` already establishes elsewhere in the codebase.

Both are the issue #771 defect, unfixed, in another language.

### 2. The issue body's markdown was broken

`report_issue::compose_report` inlined every turn into a bullet:

```rust
body.push_str("- **");  body.push_str(role);  body.push_str(":** ");
body.push_str(text.trim());          // multi-line text escapes the list item
```

Any turn containing a newline — which every real assistant answer does — broke out of the list item. The fetched page's own headings and lists then rendered as top-level issue content, and the role attribution was lost after the first line. Nothing bounded the size either.

**Fix:** render each turn as a bold role label followed by a blockquote, quoting blank lines too so a paragraph break cannot end the quote early. The transcript is bounded per turn and overall, with a seed-declared notice when trimmed, so the body always fits GitHub's 65536-character issue limit.

## Reproducing and verifying

`cargo run --example issue_771_report_format` replays the reported session and prints what a human would receive.

**Before** — 51KB, transcript escaped into top-level markdown ([the filed issue](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/771)):

```markdown
- **assistant:** Частные космические компании за рубежом | ТЕХНОЛОГИИ, ИНЖИНИРИНГ...
Главное меню Перейти к основному содержимому О нас Новости
Наука Техника Инжиниринг Промышленность Инновации Видео
... 1500 more unattributed lines of site chrome ...
```

**After** — 565 bytes, every turn attributed and contained:

```markdown
Reported from an agentic session via natural language (no web UI available).

### Conversation

**user:**

> В каких странах есть частные космические компании?

**assistant:**

> Частные космические компании работают в США, Великобритании, Германии, Франции, Испании, Китае и Индии.
>
> Source: https://integral-russia.ru/2026/03/06/chastnye-kosmicheskie-kompanii/

**user:**

> report

Filed automatically by Formal AI in agentic mode.
```

## Tests

`tests/unit/issue_771.rs` — written first, and confirmed to fail against the unfixed code (4 of 8 red, reproducing both defects exactly as reported). One test per requirement plus a whole-task test that replays the reported session:

| Test | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| `does_not_dump_the_whole_fetched_page` | answer is an extract, chrome and boilerplate gone |
| `every_supported_language_gets_an_extract_and_not_a_page_dump` | the same, in en / ru / hi / zh |
| `keeps_the_sentence_that_answers_the_question_and_cites_the_source` | the answering sentence survives, source cited |
| `a_short_page_is_still_answered_verbatim` | #687's concise case is not regressed |
| `a_multiline_turn_does_not_escape_its_block` | no transcript line renders as top-level markdown |
| `each_turn_is_attributed_to_its_role` | continuation lines stay with their turn |
| `a_huge_transcript_stays_within_the_github_body_limit` | body fits GitHub's limit |
| `an_exhausted_transcript_budget_says_so_instead_of_truncating_silently` | a trimmed transcript states that it was trimmed |
| `the_intro_heading_and_footer_still_frame_the_transcript` | existing framing preserved |
| `the_reported_session_files_a_readable_issue` | whole task, end to end |

Per CONTRIBUTING requirement 6, `experiments/agent_cli_e2e/run_issue_771.sh` drives the same session through the **real Agent CLI** against a PATH-local fake `gh`, asserting on the captured body that every turn stays attributed and contained and that the body fits the limit. Wired into `release.yml`. Its prompts differ from `run_issue_687.sh` so a pass proves general routing rather than a memorised phrasing. It passes end to end against the live web: **the filed body is around 1.1k characters** (1161 locally, 1075 in CI — it varies with what the live web returns), against the 51KB body in the issue.

One harness detail worth flagging: the fetched page is far larger than the Agent CLI's context budget, so the client prunes and summarizes the session before serving the next prompt, and the turn that triggers that pruning is consumed by it rather than reaching the server. That is client behaviour we do not control, so the harness asks again (up to three attempts) and pins what it set out to pin — that a report request lands and that the body it produces is well-formed — rather than how many prompts the client spends reorganising its own history first.

## Notes

- The new seed string `issue_report_transcript_trimmed` is grounded through `scripts/close-total.py`; `audit-total-closure.py` reports `unresolved_distinct: 0`.
- `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy --all-targets` are clean; the full suite passes (1811 unit + 150 integration).
- Two assertions were measuring `body.len()` (bytes) against GitHub's 65536-**character** limit. The reported session is Russian, where every character costs two bytes, so the byte count overstated the body by ~80%. They now measure characters, as the E2E harness already did with `wc -m`.
- **Out of scope, found while testing and worth its own issue:** the English question *"Which countries have private space companies?"* is not routed to research at all. It resolves to `intent=agent_suggestion` — "It looks like you want to run a terminal command" — because `which` is a seeded Unix command token (`data/seed/terminal-commands.lino:122`). The Russian, Hindi and Chinese equivalents all route correctly, so this is an English-only misrouting. The test uses *"What countries…"* to stay on topic; I have not touched the terminal-command detector here.
title: fix(agentic): make agentic issue reports readable and bounded
